source.title: Clinical validation of droplet digital PCR assays in detecting BRAF V600 -mutant circulating tumour DNA as a prognostic biomarker in patients with resected stage III melanoma receiving adjuvant therapy (COMBI-AD): a biomarker analysis from a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial.

finding.description: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse recurrence-free survival in the placebo group with a median of 3.71 months compared to 24.41 months.
finding.effect_summary: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse recurrence-free survival in the placebo group.

source_quote: As a binary variable, ctDNA detection was associated with worse recurrence-free survival (placebo group: median 3·71 months [95% CI 2·39-6·89] vs 24·41 months [17·28-43·13]; hazard ratio [HR] 2·91 [95% CI 1·99-4·25], p<0·0001)

source.title: Clinical validation of droplet digital PCR assays in detecting BRAF V600 -mutant circulating tumour DNA as a prognostic biomarker in patients with resected stage III melanoma receiving adjuvant therapy (COMBI-AD): a biomarker analysis from a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial.

finding.description: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse recurrence-free survival in the combination therapy group with a median of 16.59 months compared to 68.11 months.
finding.effect_summary: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse recurrence-free survival in the combination therapy group.

source_quote: combination therapy group: median 16·59 months [95% CI 12·02-26·80] vs 68·11 months [50·36-not reached]; HR 2·98 [1·95-4·54], p<0·0001)

source.title: Clinical validation of droplet digital PCR assays in detecting BRAF V600 -mutant circulating tumour DNA as a prognostic biomarker in patients with resected stage III melanoma receiving adjuvant therapy (COMBI-AD): a biomarker analysis from a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial.

finding.description: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse overall survival in the placebo group with a median of 33.90 months compared to not reached.
finding.effect_summary: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse overall survival in the placebo group.

source_quote: overall survival (placebo group: median 33·90 months [13·96-not reached] vs not reached; HR 3·35 [2·01-5·55], p<0·0001)

source.title: Clinical validation of droplet digital PCR assays in detecting BRAF V600 -mutant circulating tumour DNA as a prognostic biomarker in patients with resected stage III melanoma receiving adjuvant therapy (COMBI-AD): a biomarker analysis from a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial.

finding.description: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse overall survival in the combination therapy group with a median of 40.31 months compared to not reached.
finding.effect_summary: Baseline ctDNA detection was associated with worse overall survival in the combination therapy group.

source_quote: combination therapy group: median 40·31 months [24·90-not reached] vs not reached; HR 4·27 [2·50-7·27], p<0·0001)

source.title: Clinical validation of droplet digital PCR assays in detecting BRAF V600 -mutant circulating tumour DNA as a prognostic biomarker in patients with resected stage III melanoma receiving adjuvant therapy (COMBI-AD): a biomarker analysis from a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial.

finding.description: Patients with adverse longitudinal ctDNA kinetics had markedly shorter median recurrence-free survival of 8.31 months compared to those with favourable kinetics.
finding.effect_summary: Adverse longitudinal ctDNA kinetics were associated with shorter recurrence-free survival.

source_quote: Patients with adverse longitudinal ctDNA kinetics (molecular relapse or persistently positive) had markedly shorter median recurrence-free survival (8·31 months [95% CI 5·39-12·20] and 5·32 months [2·79-not reached], respectively) compared with patients with favourable kinetics (ie, undetectable after positive baseline result: 19·25 months [16·39-not reached]; and durable undetectable: not reached [38·44-not reached], p<0·0001).
